selected_important_turn.text: That sounds like a fair compromise. I can get food by hunting animals, old school style. That would give you 4 total items. In that case it's only fair if I take all 3 of the firewood. 4 for me and 4 for you sounds like a good deal to me. 🙂
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is the key turning point: after the opponent made a prosocial offer that traded food for water, you accept that split but immediately claim all 3 firewood for yourself. That shifts the interaction from cooperative information-exchange to a competitive allocation and determines the final item distribution.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although framed as "fair" (4/4), taking all firewood unilaterally secures your top-priority good and risks appearing selfish — it converts a cooperative trade into a zero-sum claim and could have been handled more collaboratively. It closes off integrative options (e.g., sharing/compensating on firewood) and unnecessarily increases the chance of resentment despite the deal eventually being accepted.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Rather than insisting on all 3 firewood outright, stay in the prosocial frame: propose a contingent or partial claim (e.g., offer to take 2 firewood now and hunt/provide something in return, or ask if they’re comfortable giving you firewood and offer compensation). If you really need more firewood, explain the importance and ask for a trade that is explicit and compensatory instead of an absolute take.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (by opening room for compensating trades and avoiding locking resources)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (less likely to provoke resistance or silent resentment)

value2reason.Low: The temperature might drop during the night and I want to keep my camp warm.
value2reason.Medium: I am allergic to some of the food that was provided so I need additional choices.
value2reason.High: One of my family members decided to join us last minute and now we no longer have enough water for everyone.

value2reason.Low: Food is more of a luxury instead of a necessity. You need water to survive.
value2reason.Medium: It might be hot outside which leads to thirst. Water is important for staying hydrated.
value2reason.High: Firewood is needed because campfires are pleasant and firewood only lasts a finite amount of time

dialogue.text: Hello there I was just thinking of the upcoming camping trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm sorry about that, accidentally hit that send button lol! I was thinking about it as well. I'm pretty excited. How about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I was just thinking about the logistics and how to prioritize the various items.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Same here. I'll go ahead and list some of what I need and you let me know if you will need the same as well. I need some extra water for our camping trip as well as some additional food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds reasonable. I don't particularly need the food because I see that as more of a luxury but I could really use some more water. It's supposed to get really really hot on the weekend. 😮
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I hear that. What do you think of if I take 3 of the food supplies and you can have two waters?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds like a fair compromise. I can get food by hunting animals, old school style. That would give you 4 total items. In that case it's only fair if I take all 3 of the firewood. 4 for me and 4 for you sounds like a good deal to me. 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I appreciate the offer, but I think we can do better. Since water is my highest priority, I’m willing to trade some food for an extra water package. How about I take 2 waters, 1 food, and 1 firewood, and you take the remaining 1 water, 2 foods, and 2 firewoods? It’s fair and ensures we both have enough water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I accept the split. I’ll take 2 waters, 1 food, and 1 firewood. You can have the remaining 1 water, 2 foods, and 2 firewoods. Let’s make sure we’re both set for the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Great to have reached an agreement! Let's make the most of our camping trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
